title: Youth Skills Training And Employment Outcome Gaps In Low-Income Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for mapping youth skills-training availability and estimating employment-outcome effectiveness at sub-national scale in LMICs, with explicit training-provider data fragmentation documentation, employment-outcome tracking limitations, and the distinction between training enrollment and verified employment impact.
why_it_matters: ILO estimates 67 million unemployed youth globally, with youth unemployment rates 3-4x adult rates in many LMICs. Skills-training programs are a standard policy response, but systematic reviews find average employment effects of only 2-5 percentage points, with enormous heterogeneity by program design and labor-market context. Employment-outcome tracking is conducted by fewer than 10 percent of training programs in most LMICs. Without verified outcome data, skills-training investment cannot be evidence-targeted where it would most reduce youth unemployment.

success_metrics.target: Every training-availability claim documents provider-registry fragmentation, data sources, and coverage gaps across government, NGO, and private training operators.
success_metrics.validation_method: Schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against provider-data documentation.

success_metrics.target: No training-effectiveness claim is merged without documenting employment-outcome tracking rates and the distinction between training enrollment and verified employment impact.
success_metrics.validation_method: Reviewer confirms outcome-tracking rate documentation and enrollment-vs-impact distinction.

success_metrics.target: Every training-gap analysis documents local labor-market conditions including informal-sector employment, employer demand, and skills-mismatch indicators.
success_metrics.validation_method: Red-team reviewer confirms labor-market context documentation for every training-gap claim.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, outcome-tracking safety review, and replication for quantitative outputs.
